name: @forwardimpact/gemba
description: Stand up and operate an agent team — the runtime platform's command family (harness, trace, benchmark, selfedit, wiki, xmr) and CI actions, consuming the runtime libraries.
author: D. Olsson <hi@senzilla.io>

jobs.goal: Stand Up and Operate an Agent Team
jobs.trigger: The team wants to run coding agents continuously, but the runtime — bootstrap, session harness, traces, memory, metrics — has no product to hire and must be reverse-engineered from CI plumbing.
jobs.bigHire: stand up and operate an agent team on one platform: bootstrap the environment, run sessions, inspect traces, persist memory, and measure outcomes.
jobs.littleHire: run one agent session, read one trace, or chart one metric with a single command from the platform's family.
jobs.competesWith: hand-rolled CI pipelines around raw agent CLIs; reverse-engineering another team's setup; running agents ad hoc with no memory or measurement

forces.push: Every team that wants to run agents rebuilds the same bootstrap-run-remember-measure loop from CI plumbing.
forces.pull: One installable platform whose CLIs and CI actions are the same loop, proven daily by a reference tenant.
forces.habit: Wiring raw agent CLIs into bespoke pipelines and losing the session evidence.
forces.anxiety: Adopting a platform might couple the team to one vendor's agent workflow.

jobs.firedWhen: the platform's loop constrains a team more than the plumbing it replaced; or the team stops running agents in CI.
